questions.statement: Two balls of masses 2 kg and 4 kg are moving with acceleration 5 m/s<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;and 12.5 m/s<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;respectively. Find acceleration of centre of mass.

questions.statement: The direction of the magnetic field at a point due to an infinitely long wire carrying current is:
options: Along the perpendicular drawn from a point on the wire /
Perpendicular to the plane containing the conductor and the point /

questions.statement: Which of the following statement is incorrect?
options: Acceleration due to gravity is zero at centre of earth /
Acceleration due to gravity increased linearly as we move from centre to surface of the earth /
Acceleration due to gravity is maximum at planet&rsquo;s surface /
Acceleration due to gravity is maximum at centre of earth /

Part B will be more than part A /

questions.statement:
<p>Statement A:&nbsp;The temperature of the gas remains unaltered when it undergoes an adiabatic expansion.</p>

<p>Statement B: Heat energy is exchanged between a system and its surrounding&nbsp;during an adiabatic process.</p>
options: Both A and B are true /
Both A and B are false /

questions.statement: A convex lens of focal length 8 cm is kept in contact with a concave lens of focal length 10 cm. What will be the focal length of this combination?

<p>Assertion: The kinetic energy, with any reference, must be positive.</p>

<p>Reason: In the expression for kinetic energy, the velocity appears with power 2.</p>
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true. Reason is the correct&nbsp;explanation of assertion. /
Both Assertion and Reason are true. Reason is not the correct&nbsp;explanation of assertion. /
Assertion is true and reason is false /
Both assertion and reason are false /
